questions.question: Explain the difference between relation schema and relation instance.
questions.answer: Relation schema defines the structure: relation name, attribute names, and their domains (data types). Example: Student(ID: INT, Name: VARCHAR, GPA: DECIMAL). Relation instance is the actual data (set of tuples) at a given time. Schema is stable; instance changes with INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations.

questions.question: What is the difference between Select (σ) and Project (π) operations?
questions.answer: Select (σ) filters ROWS based on a condition - it reduces cardinality. σ_GPA>3.5(Student) returns only students with high GPA. Project (π) chooses COLUMNS - it reduces degree. π_Name(Student) returns only the Name column. Select is horizontal, Project is vertical.

questions.question: Write relational algebra for: 'Names of students enrolled in CS101'.
questions.answer: π_Name(σ_CourseID='CS101'(Student ⋈ Enrollment)). First, join Student and Enrollment tables (⋈). Then, filter for CS101 (σ). Finally, project only Name column (π).

questions.question: What are the properties of a relation?
questions.answer: 1) Atomic values - each cell has single value (1NF). 2) No duplicate tuples - all rows unique. 3) Tuple order doesn't matter. 4) Attribute order doesn't matter. 5) Unique attribute names within relation. These properties distinguish relations from arbitrary tables.
